prompt: I'm tired of manually joining across tetrode sort groups every time I want all my hippocampal spikes. How do I make this easier?
behavioral_checks: Recommends `SortedSpikesGroup` as the existing aggregation (don't loop sort_group_ids)

DERIVES the hippocampal sort_group_ids by walking `SortGroup.SortGroupElectrode * Electrode * BrainRegion` filtered by `region_name`, AND restricts the merge IDs by those sort_group_ids before passing to `create_group(...)` — does NOT pass all session merge_ids unfiltered (which would defeat the point of the eval)
Names `UnitSelectionParams` as the parameter table that filters unit labels within the group, with `'all_units'` / `'exclude_noise'` defaults

Builds keys with the projected attr name `spikesorting_merge_id` (NOT bare `merge_id`) because `SortedSpikesGroup.Units` part FKs to `SpikeSortingOutput.proj(spikesorting_merge_id='merge_id')` (group.py:73). Calls `SpikeSortingOutput().get_restricted_merge_ids(key, ...)` with a **single dict per call** — looping per recording key and extending the list — because the source raises `TypeError` on a list arg (spikesorting_merge.py:138).
Explains why this is reusable: once the group exists, downstream Computed tables FK to the group key, not to tetrode-level merge IDs
